priorities.label: S97 P1 — owl-b stray-flame open issue: the veo owl-b loop has an intermittent fire/ember licking in at the side edges + bottom, leaking through the (full-width) shared spirit-mask.
priorities.objective: Remove the stray flame visible at owl-b's mask edges (S96 open issue) WITHOUT degrading the (lovely) owl+lantern loop or touching the other 3 spirits.
priorities.scope: Ship a tighter owl-b-SPECIFIC render mask (public/art/nonet/owl-b-mask.png — the shared mask's alpha linear-stretched so alpha<~80 -> 0, >~175 -> 255: the flame regions (measured alpha 19-57) crush to transparent, the owl body/wings (alpha 100-231) stay bright). Wire an optional per-Guardian `mask` field in sudokuOverlay.ts; owl-b uses owl-b-mask.png, the other 3 keep spirit-mask.png. The owl-b.webm VIDEO is UNCHANGED (no re-encode, no veo re-shoot — the loop is good). OUT: re-shooting via veo; tightening the shared mask (would affect kami/owl-a/moss-b); baked video darkening (rejected — it dimmed the whole owl).
priorities.testing: Masked-composite render verification DONE (ffmpeg: original owl-b.webm through owl-b-mask.png over dusk -> owl bright, flame gone). tsc 0; vitest green (render-only, no node-tested path); build + bundle guard (PNG is an off-bundle asset); LIVE preview: owl-b clean + other 3 spirits unchanged + 0 console errors.
priorities.check_method: S97 P1: owl-b stray-flame cropped via a tighter per-guardian matte. DIAGNOSIS (empirical): extracted owl-b.webm frames + composited through the EXACT shared spirit-mask via ffmpeg (alphaextract+alphamerge over dusk) — confirmed a warm fire/ember flares cyclically at the side edges + bottom and IS visible through the matte (the prior 'cropped' note was optimistic). Sampled the shared mask alpha profile: flame regions sit at alpha 19-57, the owl body/wings/lantern at 100-231, and the mask is full-width (alpha>30 reaches x=0..540) — feathered only top/bottom — so the lower/side flame leaks. REJECTED two approaches empirically: (a) re-process owl-b from raw with a baked tightened matte over black — killed the flame but DIMMED the whole owl (multiplied the body, not just edges); (b) veo re-shoot — gambles the (lovely) loop + costs $. SHIPPED: an owl-b-SPECIFIC tighter render matte (public/art/nonet/owl-b-mask.png = the shared mask's alpha linear-stretched via ffmpeg lutyuv y='clip((val-80)*255/95,0,255)': flame alpha 19-57 -> 0, owl alpha >175 -> 255, smooth feather 80-175) + an optional per-Guardian `mask` field in sudokuOverlay.ts (owl-b -> owl-b-mask, the other 3 default to spirit-mask byte-identically). The owl-b.webm VIDEO is UNCHANGED. GATES: tsc 0; vitest 1488/1488; build+bundle guard green (entry FLAT 553.6/560 — owl-b-mask.png is an off-bundle public asset, sudokuOverlay chunk unchanged at 12.08kB); masked-composite render shows owl bright + lantern intact + flame gone; LIVE overlay introspection (dev import of SudokuOverlay + render(world) with sudoku active) confirms IN-CONTEXT wiring: owl-b.webm is masked by owl-b-mask.png, kami/owl-a/moss-b by spirit-mask.png, 0 failed requests, 0 console errors. Committed 77b7dbd, pushed.

priorities.label: S97 P2 — G3b Combo Codex: title-screen combo encyclopedia (Magic-14), discovered combos reveal (name+recipe+silhouette), undiscovered render as locked silhouettes; discoveries persist across matches.
priorities.objective: Close the discovery loop (BACKLOG Tier-1 G3b): a title-screen Codex that marks discovered combos and renders undiscovered ones as silhouettes. Today only a GODLY codex exists (codexOverlay.ts) and combo discovery is PER-MATCH (world.discoveredCombos is cleared on START_GAME / RETURN_TO_TITLE) — so this needs both a combo codex AND cross-match persistence.
priorities.scope: (1) New lazy comboCodexStore.ts (localStorage 'spark:combos:discovered:v1' — load/merge/persist a Set<ComboKey>, mirroring codexStore.ts). (2) Merge world.discoveredCombos -> the store at the render boundary (host AND client mirror both persist their own view) so it survives the per-match clear + abrupt quit. (3) New lazy comboCodexOverlay.ts: a grid of the Magic-14 (MAGIC_COMBO_KEYS) — each tile draws the combo silhouette via the existing bondVisual silhouette helpers (drawWheel/drawStar/drawAnchor/...); DISCOVERED -> full-color silhouette + resultName + recipe (e.g. 'Triangle -> Circle'); UNDISCOVERED -> dim/desaturated silhouette + '???' (shape teased, recipe hidden = brother-surprise, the codexOverlay convention). (4) Title-screen 'COMBOS' access button (titleScreen.ts) + main.ts lazy wire. (5) Tests: store load/merge/persist; tile discovered-vs-locked state; Magic-14 completeness. OUT: any combo SEMANTICS/balance change (that is the PARKED order-symmetry PDR); functional-placeholder combos (codex shows only the discoverable Magic-14); a protocol/schema bump (none — discoveredCombos already rides the snapshot since S88).
priorities.testing: tsc 0; full vitest green + new comboCodexStore + tile-state tests; build + bundle guard (overlay is lazy-loaded -> off the main entry, like CodexOverlay); LIVE preview: open Combo Codex from title (undiscovered = locked silhouettes), play a match forming a magic combo, return to title -> that combo now REVEALED + persisted across reload; 0 console errors. Ultracode adversarial review workflow (correctness + persistence-determinism + 1v1-mirror + bundle) before commit.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #tighter-render-mask-beats-baked-darken-or-reshoot-for-a-stray-matte-edge-artifact: a localized generative artifact (a cyclic flame veo baked into the owl-b loop) was leaking through the shared full-width spirit-mask. THREE fix routes considered + two rejected EMPIRICALLY: baking a tightened matte into the video dimmed the whole subject (it multiplies the body, not just the edges); a veo re-shoot gambles a good loop + costs $. The winner was a per-asset TIGHTER RENDER MASK (crop) — the subject stays full-brightness inside the matte, only the offending edge band is cropped, the source video is untouched, $0, deterministic, and scoped to the one asset via an optional per-entry mask field (others default byte-identically). LESSONS: (a) to size the crop, MEASURE — sample the mask alpha where the artifact sits (flame 19-57) vs the subject (100-231) and stretch the alpha to zero the gap (lutyuv clip); (b) verify a matte fix with an ffmpeg masked-composite (alphaextract+alphamerge over the real bg) — it is PIXEL-EQUIVALENT to Pixi's alpha mask and far more precise + reliable than a heavy WebGL screenshot (preview_screenshot timed out on the 1920x1080 canvas); (c) prove the wiring IN-CONTEXT without a screenshot by dev-importing the overlay, render()-ing it, and introspecting which mask texture each video sprite carries (owl-b.webm <- owl-b-mask.png, others <- spirit-mask.png).
